compiled_state.title: brainstorm SKILL.md: Intent Gap Analysis displays 'skip and proceed' prompt on every gap question -> unnecessary friction
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Expected Behavior
The Intent Gap Analysis (Phase 1 Gate Step 2) should only display the skip prompt when the question is genuinely optional and the user might want to bypass it. During brainstorm sessions where the user has already confirmed intent and the gap question is about implementation approach (not intent clarification), the skip prompt adds noise.

## Actual Behavior
Every gap question includes the boilerplate: '(You can skip and proceed — just say "proceed" to continue)' regardless of whether the question is substantive or perfunctory. This is a template artifact from the SKILL.md that gets rendered verbatim even when the question is clearly relevant to approach design.

## Impact
Minor UX friction — the prompt suggests the question is optional when it may actually be important for approach selection.
